versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 1 Then Moses summoned all Israel and said to them:
Hear, O Israel, the statutes and ordinances that I declare in your hearing this day. Learn them and observe them carefully. 2 The LORD our God made a covenant with us at Horeb.
3 He did not make this covenant with our fathers, but with all of us who are alive here today. 4 The LORD spoke with you face to face out of the fire on the mountain.

context_lines: In **Deuteronomy 5**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Deuteronomy.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Covenant at Horeb** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Deuteronomy 5** remains the first control on meaning.
